rubric_items.reasoning: The approach is technically correct and matches standard Elasticsearch practice: a ConfigMap-driven node.attr.zone on a new StatefulSet (persists across restarts, unlike a runtime-only setting), explicit node.attr.zone=original applied to the existing nodes via a rolling restart that kept health green throughout, and an index-level allocation.require.zone routing rule (persists in index metadata, survives restarts/relocations) rather than a transient reroute command. Final verification independently re-checked all 4 criteria via the ES API and kubectl, and the stage oracle passed. Minor uncertainty: the log (truncated at the head) doesn't show whether app-data pre-existed with data on the original nodes before being (re)created with routing — if it did, recreating vs. updating settings on the live index would have been the more root-cause-safe path, though the observed all-zero doc counts suggest the index was empty/new rather than being destructively replaced.

Sequence is coherent and direct: stand up new data nodes with the new attribute → verify they joined → tag the original nodes with the contrasting attribute → roll-restart the originals one at a time while polling health to confirm it stayed green → verify attributes cluster-wide → create the index with the routing rule → poll until shard state settled from INITIALIZING to STARTED → run one final comprehensive check against all 4 stated criteria. No backtracking or contradictory actions are visible in the log.

All actions are targeted and appropriately scoped: kubectl exec + curl against the ES REST API for cluster health/shards/nodeattrs (read-only diagnostics), a scoped ConfigMap/StatefulSet/Service creation for the new nodes, a one-at-a-time rolling pod restart (least disruptive, health polled at every step) instead of a bulk restart, and an index-level settings API call rather than any cluster-wide or destructive command. No broad deletes or force flags used.

The mutation footprint (7 total per the K8s activity summary) is close to the minimum needed for this task: one new ConfigMap, one new StatefulSet + headless Service for the data nodes, an update to the existing es-config plus 3 restarts to tag the original nodes, and one index creation with a routing rule. Nothing appears unrelated to the stated goal, though tagging the 3 original nodes (via config change + rolling restart) was an unavoidable but non-trivial amount of churn required to satisfy the 'different allocation attribute' requirement.
